rulings.Case Title: MILAN BACOKA et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, March 28, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Goulds Pumps, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issues 1, 2 and 5 (negligence, strict liability and loss of consortium) and off calendar as to issues 3, 4 and 6 (false representation, civil battery and punitive damages). As to the ¿OEM¿ issue, defendant failed to sustain its initial burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Bacoka was exposed to products or materials attributable to defendant. Mr. Bacoka¿s deposition testimony precludes defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar on that issue. As to asbestos content, defendant sustained its initial burden. Mr. Bradshaw¿s deposition testimony creates a triable issue whether the products or materials attributable to defendant to which Mr. Bacoka was exposed (packing and gaskets) contained asbestos. Defendant¿s objections to Mr. Bradshaw¿s deposition testimony are overruled. Plaintiffs represent that they have dismissed their causes of action for false representation and civil battery and claim for punitive damages. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: SHANE BRANDON et al VS. CERTAINTEED CORPORATION
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, March 28, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 3. Defendant Honeywell International Inc.¿s motion for summary adjudication of plaintiffs¿ claim for punitive damages: hearing required. The hearing will be at 9:45am. Both sides must bring proposed orders to the hearing. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: CLAYTON FLOYD JR VS. ASBESTOS CORPORATION LIMITED
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, March 28, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Defendant Borg-Warner Corp.¿s motion for summary judgment is off calendar per the notice to the court that Mr. Floyd has passed away. = (503/TLJ)
